page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores using the arm64 architecture, powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors operating at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration delivers 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local data storage, the instance is equipped with 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read and write operations for both small and large blocks ranking in the top tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier throughput, registering 197.57 million operations per second in Redis testing. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and gzip single-threaded compression scoring in the average tier.

The instance exhibits a notable tradeoff in large language model inference tasks; while text generation speeds for medium and large models rank in the top tier, text generation for small models falls into the bottom 25%. Given the high memory density, substantial local NVMe storage, and strong multi-threaded capabilities, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching layers, and software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, including 197.57 million operations per second in Redis SET testing. While single-core capabilities and small-model LLM text generation are average to weak, the instance excels in multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and large-model text generation. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.24xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 96 physical cores, 768 GB RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance combining ninety-six physical cores with high-capacity local NVMe storage for data-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated physical cores on AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors
768.0 GB of system memory paired with 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth supported by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
